focus: Say who you are, who someone is, and where people are from.
rule: Polish often drops the subject pronoun because the verb ending already shows the person. Use the pronoun for contrast or clarity.
pattern: ja jestem, ty jesteś, on/ona jest, my jesteśmy, wy jesteście, oni/one są

check.hint: Third-person singular uses jest.

focus: Give your name and ask someone else for their name politely.
rule: The small word się marks a reflexive verb. It usually follows the conjugated verb or another stressed word.
pattern: nazywam się, nazywasz się, nazywa się, nazywamy się, nazywacie się, nazywają się

examples.english: What is your name? (formal to a woman)
What is your name? (formal to a man)

check.hint: The reflexive marker stays with nazywać się.

topic: Questions with jak, kto, co, skąd
focus: Build the first useful questions for identity, objects, and origin.
rule: Question words usually come near the beginning. Czy turns a statement into a yes/no question.
pattern: Jak...? Kto...? Co...? Skąd...? Czy...?

check.hint: Ask about origin with skąd.

focus: Say where someone is from and recognize country names in forms.
rule: After być z meaning “to be from”, the place normally takes genitive: Polska -> z Polski, Ukraina -> z Ukrainy.
pattern: Jestem z + genitive place.

check.hint: Use z to mean from.

focus: Talk about family, documents, and everyday things you have.
rule: Mieć means “to have”. The thing you have often appears in accusative, but many common family phrases can be learned as whole chunks first.
pattern: mam, masz, ma, mamy, macie, mają

examples.english: I have a daughter and a son.

check.hint: First-person singular of mieć is mam.

topic: Noun gender and ten, ta, to
focus: Recognize masculine, feminine, and neuter nouns for classroom and form-filling vocabulary.
rule: Many masculine nouns end in a consonant, many feminine nouns end in -a, and many neuter nouns end in -o or -e. There are exceptions, so learn nouns with ten, ta, or to.
pattern: ten adres, ta ulica, to imię

check.hint: Ulica is usually feminine.

focus: Describe people, feelings, clothing, rooms, and food with matching endings.
rule: An adjective changes to match the noun: masculine -y/-i, feminine -a, neuter -e.
pattern: dobry lekarz, dobra kawa, dobre mieszkanie

check.hint: Kawa is feminine.

focus: Say your job, role, or profession.
rule: After jestem meaning “I am a/an” with a profession or role, use instrumental: student -> studentem, studentka -> studentką.
pattern: Jestem + instrumental profession.

check.hint: Masculine profession in instrumental often ends in -em.

focus: Handle common verbs from daily routine lessons.
rule: Many everyday verbs follow repeatable endings. Learn each verb with the first two forms to know its group.
pattern: mieszkam/mieszkasz, rozumiem/rozumiesz, mówię/mówisz, uczę się/uczysz się

check.hint: Mówić uses mówię for I speak.

focus: Ask for food, items, and clothing in shops.
rule: A direct object often takes accusative. Feminine -a often becomes -ę: kawa -> kawę, bułka -> bułkę.
pattern: Poproszę + accusative. Mam + accusative. Lubię + accusative.

check.hint: Feminine -a usually becomes -ę in accusative singular.

focus: Say what you like to eat, drink, and do.
rule: Use lubię + infinitive for activities, or lubię + noun in accusative for things. Wolę compares preferences.
pattern: Lubię jeść..., Lubię kawę, Wolę herbatę niż kawę.

check.hint: After lubię, an action stays in the infinitive.

focus: Say what you do not have or what is missing.
rule: Nie before a verb negates the action. After nie mam, Polish commonly uses genitive: czas -> czasu, karta -> karty.
pattern: Nie rozumiem. Nie mam + genitive.

examples.english: I do not have a card.

check.hint: After nie mam, masculine bilet becomes biletu.

focus: Say where things are in a room, town, school, or office.
rule: After location words w and na, use locative: Polska -> w Polsce, stół -> na stole, biuro -> w biurze.
pattern: w/na + locative place

examples.english: The book is lying on the table.

check.hint: Use w for being in school.

focus: Understand and give simple route instructions.
rule: Proszę + infinitive is a polite command. Direction chunks are often learned as fixed phrases first.
pattern: Proszę iść prosto. Proszę skręcić w prawo.

examples.english: The pharmacy is on the right side.

check.hint: After proszę as a polite command, use the infinitive.

topic: Motion verbs iść, jechać, chodzić, jeździć
focus: Choose walking vs riding and one-time vs repeated movement.
rule: Iść means going on foot now or once. Jechać means going by transport now or once. Chodzić/jeździć describe repeated movement.
pattern: Idę do sklepu. Jadę autobusem. Chodzę do szkoły. Jeżdżę tramwajem.

examples.english: I go by tram to school regularly.

check.hint: One trip by transport uses jechać: jadę.

focus: Talk about opening hours, schedules, and routines.
rule: Use w + day for on Monday etc. Use od + genitive and do + genitive for from... to....
pattern: w poniedziałek, o ósmej, od ósmej do szesnastej

examples.polish: Urząd jest otwarty od ósmej do szesnastej.
examples.english: The office is open from eight to sixteen.

check.prompt: Sklep jest otwarty ___ dziewiątej do siedemnastej.
check.hint: From...to... is od...do....

focus: Describe basic health problems at a doctor or pharmacy.
rule: Use boli mnie for one painful thing and bolą mnie for plural painful things. Use mam + symptom for common symptoms.
pattern: Boli mnie głowa. Bolą mnie plecy. Mam gorączkę.

check.hint: Gardło is singular, so use boli.

focus: Describe what someone is wearing and ask for clothes in a shop.
rule: Mam na sobie takes accusative. Nosić means to wear or carry habitually.
pattern: Mam na sobie + accusative clothing. Noszę + accusative clothing.

examples.english: I am wearing a blue jacket.
Can I try on this shirt?

check.hint: Kurtkę is feminine accusative, so the adjective ends in -ą.

focus: Talk about tomorrow’s weather and near-future plans.
rule: The future of być is będę, będziesz, będzie, będziemy, będziecie, będą.
pattern: Jaka będzie pogoda? Będzie zimno. Będę w domu.

check.hint: Weather statements often use będzie.

focus: Report where you were and what you did yesterday.
rule: Past tense agrees with the speaker/person in gender and number. Masculine often uses -łem, feminine often uses -łam for “I”.
pattern: byłem/byłam, miałem/miałam, robiłem/robiłam, poszedłem/poszłam

examples.english: I was at the doctor. (female speaker)
Yesterday I went to school. (female speaker)

check.hint: Female speaker: byłam.

focus: Talk about skills, languages, and work abilities.
rule: Umieć and potrafić usually take an infinitive. Znać takes a noun: znam język polski.
pattern: Umiem pisać. Potrafię gotować. Znam angielski.

check.hint: Use znać with a language as a noun.

focus: Make requests and say what you want, can, or must do.
rule: Modal verbs are followed by an infinitive: chcę zapłacić, mogę wejść, muszę pracować.
pattern: chcę/mogę/muszę + infinitive

examples.english: I want to rent an apartment.
I have to go to the office.

check.hint: After muszę, keep the infinitive.

focus: Choose the case by question and phrase pattern instead of memorizing tables first.
rule: Start with common triggers: kto/co for nominative, mam/poproszę/lubię for accusative, nie mam/od/do/z for genitive, w/na location for locative, z/by transport/profession after jestem for instrumental.
pattern: Mianownik: kto/co. Biernik: kogo/co. Dopełniacz: kogo/czego. Miejscownik: o/w/na kim/czym. Narzędnik: z kim/czym.

examples.english: I do not have a ticket. (genitive)

check.hint: Nie mam triggers genitive.
